more.title: Rewriting the Rules Mid-Game: Why Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 Raises Profound Risks
more.description: Zimbabwe stands at a constitutional crossroads. The proposed Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3, which seeks to extend presidential and parliamentary terms from five to seven years, effectively allowing President Emmerson Mnangagwa to remain in office until 2030, represents far more than a technical legal adjustment.
